title: How Do You Celebrate America’s 250-Year Experiment? However You Want.
summary: Away from the turmoil in Washington, Americans will mark July 4 in their own patriotic ways. Expect rodeos, line dancing, Tejano music and Led Zeppelin.

title: At Mount Rushmore, Trump Veers From Patriotism to ‘Communism’
summary: On the eve of July 4, President Trump extolled the nation’s founders while branding his opponents as “communists” in what seemed to be a warm-up for November.

title: Momentary Unity at a Funeral Masks Deep Divisions Among Iran’s Leaders
summary: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei’s absence has raised questions inside Iranian political circles about who is really running the country and allowed open divisions to fester.

title: Trump Targets Not Just Georgia’s Vote, but Also Trust in Elections
summary: The president has sent 260 F.B.I. analysts to Georgia, repeating his baseless claims of fraud in 2020. But critics say the intention is to undermine overall confidence in the electoral process.

title: Trump Takes Stock of Other Presidencies, Shedding Light on His Own
summary: In his second term, the president has increasingly mused about his predecessors, comparing himself with some and distancing from the failures of others.

title: What to Know About Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Wedding Festivities
summary: Yes, they got married. Yes, they held a huge event inside Madison Square Garden. No, the guests did not post much about it. (At least they haven’t yet.)

title: Photos: See Outside Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Wedding at MSG
summary: The couple’s private celebration created a public spectacle outside Madison Square Garden.

title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s American Royal Wedding
summary: A mega-famous popstar and a Super Bowl champion celebrated their love in Midtown Manhattan, announcing both their union and their cultural domination.

title: Putin Visits Battlefield and Vows to Take More of Ukraine
summary: The Russian leader denounced Ukraine’s “imaginary achievements” on the battlefield of late, calling its leaders “play actors.”

title: America at 250
summary: The truth worth celebrating this summer is that our country is still being made.

title: The Unifying Speech We Won’t Get This Independence Day
summary: America, we need a new beginning.

title: Happy Birthday to Us
summary: A forgotten founder. A tour of Revolutionary taverns. But first, a royal wedding.

title: Did You Recently Buy a Home in New York? We Want to Hear Your Story.
summary: From the five boroughs up to the Hudson Valley and the Finger Lakes, we’re looking for home buyers to feature in our weekly column, The Hunt.

title: ‘Who Should I Vote for?’ Voters Turn to A.I. Before Casting Their Ballots
summary: It takes effort to be an informed citizen. Artificial intelligence tools offer an alluring shortcut — but they’re not without risk.
